scenarios.title: The Creative Process
scenarios.subtitle: How it starts for you.
scenarios.prompt: You're starting a new creative project. What does the beginning usually look like?

choices.text: I dive right in. I figure things out as I create
I plan it out first. Research, sketches, outlines, then I start
I have the idea but getting started is the hardest part
I wait for inspiration. I can't force it

scenarios.title: The Feedback Problem
scenarios.subtitle: Is it any good?
scenarios.prompt: You've made something and you want to know if it's good. What do you do?

choices.text: Share it with trusted people and ask for honest feedback
Post it and see how people react. Engagement tells me what I need to know
Compare it to others' work and usually feel like mine isn't as good
I don't usually share. It feels too personal

scenarios.title: The Unglamorous Work
scenarios.subtitle: The part nobody posts about.
scenarios.prompt: Every creative project has boring parts. Editing, revisions, organizing files, formatting. How do you handle it?

choices.text: I'm pretty good about it. I edit thoroughly and keep things organized
I do it but I hate every second. It's where my creative energy goes to die
I rush through it. Good enough is good enough
I avoid it and it piles up. Folders full of unedited, unorganized work

scenarios.title: The Time to Create
scenarios.subtitle: Finding it, making it, protecting it.
scenarios.prompt: When it comes to finding time for your creative work, what's the reality?

choices.text: I make it a priority. Regular schedule or routine for creating
I squeeze it in. Lunch breaks, late nights, weekends
It depends on mood and energy. Sometimes prolific, sometimes weeks go by
I don't create as much as I want to. Life gets in the way

scenarios.title: The Creative Block
scenarios.subtitle: When nothing's working.
scenarios.prompt: You're stuck. The ideas aren't coming, the work feels stale, or you can't get motivated. What do you do?

choices.text: Push through. Discipline matters more than inspiration
Step away and do something else until it passes
Consume other people's work to get inspired
I spiral. Start wondering if I'm even good at this

scenarios.title: The Tools of the Trade
scenarios.subtitle: Your creative toolkit.
scenarios.prompt: When it comes to the tools and software for your creative work, how do you feel?

choices.text: I know my tools well. Invested time in learning them
I use the basics but there's more I could learn
I feel behind. Tools and techniques I should learn but haven't
I keep things simple on purpose. Don't need fancy tools

scenarios.title: The Unfinished Projects
scenarios.subtitle: Be honest.
scenarios.prompt: Be honest. How many half-finished creative projects do you have sitting around right now?

choices.text: Not many. I finish what I start before moving on
A few. I circle back eventually
More than I'd like to admit. Great at starting, not at finishing
Everything is unfinished. I've never completed and shared something I'm proud of

scenarios.title: The Dream
scenarios.subtitle: No constraints. What does it look like?
scenarios.prompt: If you could spend your time doing exactly the creative work you want, no constraints, what would that look like?

choices.text: Close to what I'm doing. Just more of it and getting better
I'd go deeper into one thing instead of juggling multiple projects
I'd try something completely different from what I'm doing now
I don't know. I just want to create and I want it to matter
